title: Starch Concentration Protocol
author: Victor vmr Rodriguez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8gbhtsn

steps.title: Weigh out
steps.instruction: Weigh out .25 g of plant sample to be tested and add it to a 200ml beaker.

steps.title: Add 20 mL of Acetone Bio Basic Inc
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL of Acetone Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #AC1200.SIZE.1L to the test tube.

steps.title: Let the sample/acetone suspension sit in...
steps.instruction: Let the sample/acetone suspension sit in a 200 ml beaker filled with distilled water and heat water until the of Acetone Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #AC1200.SIZE.1L starts to boil.

steps.title: Let test tube cool for 00:30:00...
steps.instruction: Let test tube cool for 00:30:00 minutes to extract the pigments of the plant.

steps.title: Drain the Acetone Bio Basic Inc
steps.instruction: Drain the Acetone Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #AC1200.SIZE.1L and add 20 mL more Acetone Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #AC1200.SIZE.1L and put it back into the beaker heat water until the of Acetone Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #AC1200.SIZE.1L starts to boil.

steps.title: Let test tube cool for 00:30:00...
steps.instruction: Let test tube cool for 00:30:00 minutes more to finish extracting the pigments.

steps.title: Drain the test tube and transfer...
steps.instruction: Drain the test tube and transfer the sample to an autoclave for heating for 01:00:00 hour at 135 °C . This will loosen up the starch for extraction.

steps.title: Remove the sample from the autoclave
steps.instruction: Remove the sample from the autoclave.

steps.title: Grind the sample with a mortar...
steps.instruction: Grind the sample with a mortar and pestle until a paste.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of distilled water...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of distilled water to the pestle and wash around to dissolve the sample into a suspension and clean the pestle. Ensure all of the sample is in the suspension.

steps.title: Funnel the suspension into a test...
steps.instruction: Funnel the suspension into a test tube and add an additional 5 mL of distilled water to the test tube using the same funnel as to wash out the funnel of stuck on sample particles.

steps.title: Let stand for an hour covered
steps.instruction: Let stand for an hour covered.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of the suspension...
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of the suspension to a 3 ml vial and 1 mL of the suspension to another 3 ml vial. Mark the vials as needed for the protocol. Add 2 mL of distilled water to the vial with 1ml. This will serve as a 1/3 diluted sample in case the concentration is too high for the spectrometer to read the original sample.

steps.title: To two labeled PCR tubes, add...
steps.instruction: To two labeled PCR tubes, add 200 µL from each vial and centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 1200 x g .

steps.title: Take 100 µL from each PCR...
steps.instruction: Take 100 µL from each PCR tube and transfer it to clean PCR tubes labeled. Ensure that the sample is removed from the top to prevent sucking up settled particulates.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of distilled water...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of distilled water to a PCR tube and calibrate the Spectrometer with it.

steps.title: Insert each sample into the spectrometer
steps.instruction: Insert each sample into the spectrometer.

steps.title: The Absorbance of the samples are...
steps.instruction: The Absorbance of the samples are to be taken at the 610nm wavelength and the log base 10 of the absorbance percentage is noted.

steps.title: Use the following function to determine the concentration
steps.instruction: Use the following function to determine the concentration... C st = (A 610 *692.7 g/mol *V ml) /(2594*M sm ) With C st being the starch concentration in mg/g, A 610 is the absorbance at 610 nm, V ml is the volume in ml of the water added to the sample for extraction, and M sm is the mass in grams of the original sample taken.
